title: metastelma costatum
intro: Metastelma costatum, also known as the Mexican ballmallow, is a climbing plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. Metastelma costatum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are typically made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with other herbs.
